title: Cambridge C1 Advanced — Reading and Use of English
instructions: This is a STUB content file. Real questions will be transcribed from cae/Advanced reading practice.docx in Priority 1 of the intent plan.

parts.title: Part 1 — Multiple-choice cloze (STUB)
parts.instructions: For questions 1-8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.

passage.content: This is a placeholder passage for scaffold testing. The real CAE Part 1 passage will replace this text, complete with [[GAP:q1]] inline markers for the multiple-choice cloze.

parts.title: Part 2 — Open cloze (STUB)
parts.instructions: For questions 9-16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.

passage.content: This is a placeholder open cloze passage. The real Part 2 passage will have [[GAP:q9]] markers for single-word gap fills.

parts.title: Part 3 — Word formation (STUB)
parts.instructions: For questions 17-24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

passage.content: Placeholder passage for word formation. Real content will have inline [[GAP:q17]] markers alongside the keyword list on the right column.

parts.title: Part 4 — Key word transformation (STUB)
parts.instructions: For questions 25-30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given.

questions.prompt: My brother now earns far less money than he did when he was younger.
questions.leadIn: My brother __________ much now as he did when he was younger.

parts.title: Part 5 — Multiple choice reading (STUB)
parts.instructions: You are going to read an article. For questions 31-36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.

passage.content: Placeholder passage for multiple choice reading. The real Part 5 passage will be several paragraphs long in the left column with questions on the right.

questions.prompt: 31. What does the writer suggest in the first paragraph?

parts.title: Part 6 — Cross-text multiple matching (STUB)
parts.instructions: You are going to read four reviews of a book on de Botton's ideas. For questions 37-40, choose from the reviewers A-D.

passage.content: Placeholder for the four reviewer sections. The real Part 6 will have four labeled sections A, B, C, D in the left column.

questions.prompt: 37. has a different opinion from the others on the confidence with which de Botton discusses architecture

parts.title: Part 7 — Gapped text (STUB)
parts.instructions: You are going to read an article about Scottish wildcats. Six paragraphs have been removed from the article. Choose from the paragraphs A-G the one which fits each gap (41-46). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.

passage.content:
Scottish Wildcat

Its long bushy coat and tail are a perfect camouflage.

[[SLOT:q41]]

However, the physical differences are tangible. The wildcat is a much larger animal than a domestic tabby cat.

[[SLOT:q42]]

It is a huge range for such a small cat.

paragraphBank.text: Stealthy by nature, wildcats have been known to hunt alone for days at a stretch.
Genetic testing has confirmed the rarity of pure-bred individuals.
Yet despite its elusiveness, the Scottish wildcat is one of Britain's most iconic animals.
This is one reason conservationists have raised concerns about hybridisation.

parts.title: Part 8 — Multiple matching (STUB)
parts.instructions: You are going to read a magazine article in which five career consultants give advice about starting a career. For questions 47-56, choose from the consultants A-E.

passage.content: Placeholder for the five consultant sections. The real Part 8 will have five labeled sections A-E in the left column.

questions.prompt: 47. Keep your final objective in mind when you are planning to change jobs.
